info.name: DarkKitchen API
info.description:
Full test collection for the DarkKitchen API. Run folders in order — later folders depend on data created earlier.

PREREQUISITE: Create an Admin user directly in the DB before running, then set adminEmail and adminPassword variables.

item.name: Create Product 3 (PROD003, price 200, for promotion test)
Create Order - Express, no discount (PROD001 x2)

exec: // PROD001 x2 @ 100 each, no discount, express

item.name: Get Client Orders - filter by status

exec: pm.test('All returned orders are Pending', () => json.forEach(o => pm.expect(o.status).to.equal('Pending')));

item.name: Get Orders by Status - filter by address street
6. Business Logic - Best Discount Wins
item.description: PROD003 (price 200) is associated with two promotions: 60% and 30%. The order must apply only the highest (60%).

item.name: Create Order with PROD003 (both promotions active)

exec: // PROD003 x1 @ 200, two promotions: 60% and 30% — only 60% should apply
pm.test('Only highest discount (60%) applied — discount is 120', () => pm.expect(json.discount).to.equal(120));
pm.test('IVA calculated on discounted amount — is 17.6', () => pm.expect(json.iva).to.equal(17.6));

item.name: 7. Order Lifecycle - Delivered
item.description: Full happy path: Pending → Prepared → OnItsWay → Delivered

item.name: 10. Invalid Status Transitions
item.description: One order walks through valid transitions while testing every invalid step along the way. Terminal-state tests reuse orders from folders 7, 8, 9.

item.name: Error - Prepared → Canceled (only Pending can be canceled)
Error - Client tries to update status (403)

exec: pm.test('PROD001 is most requested (ordered 4 times total)', () => pm.expect(json[0].code).to.equal('PROD001'));
